miami.name: Miami
miami.tagline: Art deco, Latin rhythm, waterfront light.

dubai.name: Dubai
dubai.tagline: Glass towers, desert edges, old creek memory.

abu-dhabi.name: Abu Dhabi
abu-dhabi.tagline: Cultural calm, island light, ceremonial scale.

places.name: Art Deco Historic District
places.summary: Pastel facades, chrome details, and a dense first hit of Miami Beach identity.
places.story: Look upward at the building corners and neon lines. The district works best when treated like a movie set that somehow stayed alive.
places.route_logic: it gives the route an unmistakable opening image
places.swap: Use Espanola Way if you want a softer, cafe-heavy opening.

places.name: South Pointe Park
places.summary: A clean waterfront park where cruise ships, beach light, and skyline views meet.
places.story: This is the place to let the city breathe. Walk the pier slowly and watch how Miami becomes less loud at the edge.
places.route_logic: it gives a cinematic waterfront release after denser stops
places.swap: Use Venetian Causeway for a longer movement-focused ending.

places.name: Wynwood Walls
places.summary: A compact mural district built for color, street energy, and design-forward wandering.
places.story: Do not rush the famous walls. The more interesting move is to step into the side streets and let the route become less polished.
places.route_logic: it adds color and creative texture without becoming a museum stop
places.swap: Use Miami Design District for a quieter design-led version.

places.name: Miami Design District
places.summary: Luxury retail, public art, galleries, and polished architecture in a compact grid.
places.story: Treat it as an open-air design gallery. The storefronts are less important than the spaces between them.
places.route_logic: it shifts the route from beach mythology into composed contemporary design
places.swap: Use The Bass if you want more museum and less retail.

places.name: Perez Art Museum Miami
places.summary: A waterfront museum with shaded terraces, hanging gardens, and contemporary art.
places.story: The building is part of the visit. Pause under the canopy before going inside; the shade is doing half the storytelling.
places.route_logic: it gives culture, shade, and a strong architectural pause
places.swap: Use The Bass for a smaller Miami Beach museum stop.

places.name: Little Havana / Calle Ocho
places.summary: Cuban cafes, domino tables, murals, and the most audible neighborhood layer of the city.
places.story: Follow sound and smell here. Coffee, music, and conversation do more work than any single landmark.
places.route_logic: it makes the route feel lived-in instead of purely photogenic
places.swap: Use Coconut Grove if you want a slower neighborhood rhythm.

places.name: Vizcaya Museum and Gardens
places.summary: A bayfront estate where European fantasy, tropical gardens, and Miami humidity collide.
places.story: This stop feels imported and dreamlike in exactly the right way. The gardens are the emotional center.
places.route_logic: it adds a strange old-world layer to a city people often read as new
places.swap: Use Fairchild Tropical Botanic Garden for a more nature-heavy version.

places.name: Venetian Causeway
places.summary: A slow bridge sequence across Biscayne Bay with skyline views and water on both sides.
places.story: It is less a destination than a transition. The city edits itself as you cross the water.
places.route_logic: it turns transportation into a visual beat
places.swap: Use South Pointe Park if you prefer one fixed viewpoint.

places.name: Coconut Grove
places.summary: Leafy streets, cafes, marinas, and an older, slower Miami mood.
places.story: This is where the route drops its shoulders. Let the polished beach image fade into shade and neighborhood texture.
places.route_logic: it gives the day a softer local tempo
places.swap: Use Little Havana for a louder neighborhood stop.

places.name: The Bass
places.summary: A compact contemporary art museum close to Collins Park and Miami Beach hotels.
places.story: Use it as a temperature reset. A smaller museum lets the route stay nimble.
places.route_logic: it adds indoor culture without consuming the whole day
places.swap: Use PAMM if you want the museum itself to be a major architectural stop.

places.name: Espanola Way
places.summary: A narrow pedestrian street of cafes, color, and old Miami Beach theatricality.
places.story: This is a small stage, not a big landmark. It works when you let a coffee or meal become part of the route.
places.route_logic: it gives a compact food-and-walk beat near the beach
places.swap: Use Lincoln Road for a broader pedestrian corridor.

places.name: Bill Baggs Cape Florida State Park
places.summary: Beach, lighthouse, and a quieter edge of the Miami area.
places.story: Go here when the route needs air. The lighthouse makes the city feel far away without leaving the coast.
places.route_logic: it creates a quiet nature finale outside the obvious urban loop
places.swap: Use South Pointe Park for a faster coastal ending.

places.name: Burj Khalifa / Downtown Dubai
places.summary: The vertical icon of Dubai, surrounded by fountains, malls, and skyline theater.
places.story: Use it as a first shot, not the whole story. The scale is most powerful when the route later complicates it.
places.route_logic: it establishes the city as spectacle immediately
places.swap: Use Dubai Frame for a more literal old-new city metaphor.

places.name: Al Fahidi Historical Neighbourhood
places.summary: Wind towers, courtyards, galleries, and a quieter pre-skyscraper Dubai layer.
places.story: Let this stop undo the postcard version of Dubai. The city becomes smaller, cooler, and more human here.
places.route_logic: it gives historical contrast to the skyline
places.swap: Use Dubai Creek if you want the same old-city layer with more movement.

places.name: Dubai Creek / Abra Crossing
places.summary: A working waterway where old trade routes still cut through the city.
places.story: The small abra ride is the trick: a simple crossing that makes Dubai feel less abstract.
places.route_logic: it turns history into a physical transition
places.swap: Use Al Fahidi if you want to stay on foot.

places.name: Museum of the Future
places.summary: A calligraphic torus on Sheikh Zayed Road and one of Dubai's strongest future-city images.
places.story: Even from outside, the building does the job. It feels like Dubai explaining its own myth in metal and void.
places.route_logic: it makes the future theme explicit without needing a long stop
places.swap: Use Dubai Design District for a less tourist-heavy design stop.

places.name: Alserkal Avenue
places.summary: Warehouse galleries, cafes, creative studios, and a less glossy cultural side of Dubai.
places.story: This is Dubai in studio mode. The route gets more interesting when it leaves the obvious icons behind.
places.route_logic: it adds contemporary culture and local creative texture
places.swap: Use Dubai Design District for a more polished design atmosphere.

places.name: Madinat Jumeirah
places.summary: Canals, resort architecture, Burj Al Arab views, and evening atmosphere.
places.story: It is theatrical, but useful. Let it be the polished night scene after a more grounded day.
places.route_logic: it gives a controlled luxury finale with water and skyline cues
places.swap: Use Dubai Marina if you want a taller, glassier ending.

places.name: Dubai Marina Walk
places.summary: A high-rise waterfront loop with restaurants, boats, and dense night lighting.
places.story: This is the neon corridor. It works best when you arrive after the sun has lowered and the city turns reflective.
places.route_logic: it gives the route a luminous urban ending
places.swap: Use Bluewaters for a more contained waterfront stop.

places.name: Kite Beach
places.summary: A casual beach stretch with food kiosks, Burj Al Arab views, and an easier local rhythm.
places.story: This stop lowers the temperature of the route. It is where Dubai becomes sneakers, sand, and snacks.
places.route_logic: it breaks up polished urban stops with a relaxed coastal beat
places.swap: Use JBR Beach for a busier, more tourist-facing version.

places.name: Dubai Design District
places.summary: A design and fashion district with cafes, galleries, and a cleaner creative-business feel.
places.story: D3 is a useful midpoint: less spectacular than Downtown, more composed than the beach.
places.route_logic: it adds design texture while keeping the route modern
places.swap: Use Alserkal Avenue if you want art-world energy.

places.name: Dubai Frame
places.summary: A giant architectural frame that literally points between old and new Dubai.
places.story: It is a blunt metaphor, but a good one. The route can use it as a visual hinge.
places.route_logic: it connects heritage and skyline themes in one landmark
places.swap: Use Burj Khalifa for the more famous vertical icon.

places.name: Ras Al Khor Wildlife Sanctuary
places.summary: Wetlands and bird hides near the skyline, a strange quiet pocket at the city's edge.
places.story: The surprise is the point: flamingos and glass towers sharing the same field of view.
places.route_logic: it makes the city feel more layered and less predictable
places.swap: Use Alserkal Avenue if you want a quiet indoor alternative.

places.name: Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque
places.summary: A monumental white mosque of marble, symmetry, water, and ceremonial calm.
places.story: Begin here if you want Abu Dhabi to feel expansive immediately. The building is about light as much as scale.
places.route_logic: it gives the route a majestic opening image
places.swap: Use Qasr Al Watan for a palace-led ceremonial opening.

places.name: Louvre Abu Dhabi
places.summary: A museum under a geometric dome where art, shade, and sea air merge.
places.story: The dome is the mood. Let the light pattern slow the route down before moving back outside.
places.route_logic: it adds world-class culture and a shaded architectural pause
places.swap: Use Manarat Al Saadiyat for a smaller cultural stop.

places.name: Qasr Al Watan
places.summary: A working presidential palace with monumental halls, symmetry, and ceremonial detail.
places.story: This is the route's grand interior. The scale is formal, almost theatrical, but never casual.
places.route_logic: it brings ceremonial architecture into the day
places.swap: Use Emirates Palace for a hotel-and-luxury variation.

places.name: Abu Dhabi Corniche
places.summary: A long waterfront edge with skyline, beaches, bike paths, and open evening air.
places.story: The Corniche is where the route stretches out. It lets Abu Dhabi feel less formal and more lived-in.
places.route_logic: it gives the route breathing room and a clean waterfront line
places.swap: Use Saadiyat Beach for a softer nature-led ending.

places.name: Qasr Al Hosn
places.summary: The city's oldest stone building, now a heritage site in the downtown grid.
places.story: It gives Abu Dhabi a memory anchor. The route becomes more convincing when it includes this older core.
places.route_logic: it grounds the day in local history
places.swap: Use Heritage Village for a more open-air heritage stop.

places.name: Manarat Al Saadiyat
places.summary: A calm arts center on Saadiyat with exhibitions, talks, and creative community energy.
places.story: This is the less obvious cultural stop. It makes the route feel curated rather than checklist-driven.
places.route_logic: it adds quiet contemporary culture near the museum district
places.swap: Use Louvre Abu Dhabi if you want the bigger cultural centerpiece.

places.name: Jubail Mangrove Park
places.summary: Boardwalks through mangroves, shallow water, and a quiet ecological layer of the city.
places.story: This stop changes the sound of the day. The city gets replaced by water, boards, and bird movement.
places.route_logic: it gives a nature-led pause that still feels specific to Abu Dhabi
places.swap: Use Eastern Mangroves for a more urban-adjacent alternative.

places.name: Saadiyat Beach
places.summary: Pale sand, clear water, and a calmer beach scene than many Gulf city waterfronts.
places.story: Use it as the soft fade-out. After museums and palaces, the beach makes the day feel human again.
places.route_logic: it gives the route a quiet coastal finale
places.swap: Use Corniche for a more public waterfront ending.

places.name: Yas Marina
places.summary: A polished marina with restaurants, yachts, and a night-drive feel near the circuit.
places.story: This is Abu Dhabi in glossy evening mode. It works when the route wants energy without chaos.
places.route_logic: it adds a lively but controlled evening scene
places.swap: Use Corniche for a lower-budget waterfront finale.

places.name: Heritage Village
places.summary: An open-air heritage stop near the waterfront with craft and old-city references.
places.story: It is best as context, not climax. Use it to understand the jump between past and present.
places.route_logic: it adds accessible heritage before the route becomes polished again
places.swap: Use Qasr Al Hosn for a stronger historical anchor.

places.name: Umm Al Emarat Park
places.summary: A family-friendly urban park with gardens, shade, and a slower local rhythm.
places.story: This is a useful reset. It keeps the route from becoming all monuments and museums.
places.route_logic: it adds shade, softness, and a local everyday layer
places.swap: Use Jubail Mangrove Park for a more distinctive nature stop.

places.name: Observation Deck at 300
places.summary: A high, polished viewpoint across Corniche, islands, and Gulf water.
places.story: Use it when the route needs one composed final image. The city becomes a map beneath you.
places.route_logic: it creates a premium skyline finale without rushing
places.swap: Use Corniche for the same waterfront story at ground level.
